source: Invest Like the Best — AI, semiconductors, and the robotic frontier (EP.385)
summary: Networking, storage, and memory are the most underinvested and highest-opportunity layers in AI infrastructure because GPU speed has outpaced the rest of the data center 50x vs 4-5x
summary_extended: Baker argues that networking, storage, and memory represent the most underinvested and highest-opportunity layers in AI infrastructure, driven by a severe capability mismatch: GPUs have improved roughly 50x in speed over the past five years while networking, storage, and memory have only improved 4-5x. This imbalance manifests in poor cluster efficiency — model flop utilization (MFU) sits at only 35-40% despite per-GPU theoretical utilization (MAMF) of ~83%, meaning the bottleneck is not the GPU itself but the surrounding infrastructure. Baker explicitly states he is 'targeting his dollars' at next-generation networking, storage, and memory technologies, calling networking in particular 'the choke point' with the 'highest likelihood of success' as an investment. The implication is that companies supplying high-speed networking fabrics, storage solutions, and memory bandwidth for AI clusters are structurally undervalued relative to GPU-centric plays, and represent a higher-conviction infrastructure opportunity as clusters scale.
detail: Over the last five years, GPUs have gotten ~50x faster while networking, storage, and memory have only improved 4-5x. This mismatch is why MFU sits at 35-40% despite per-GPU MAMF of ~83%. Investing in next-generation networking, storage, and memory technologies—particularly networking—is the highest-conviction infrastructure opportunity because these are the binding constraints on cluster efficiency.
confidence_evidence: Baker explicitly states this is where he is 'targeting my dollars' and calls it 'the choke point' and 'highest likelihood of success'
quote_fragment: SFU is at 30 percent. Wow, that's a great place to invest

ticker_audit.reasoning: Arista Networks is the dominant supplier of high-speed Ethernet networking for AI data centers and is a direct beneficiary of Baker's thesis that networking is the binding constraint and highest-conviction infrastructure opportunity in AI clusters.

Micron is the primary US-listed producer of HBM and advanced DRAM memory, directly benefiting from Baker's thesis that memory is one of the most underinvested and highest-opportunity layers in AI infrastructure.

Pure Storage is a leading provider of high-performance storage solutions for AI workloads and directly benefits from Baker's thesis that storage is a binding constraint and underinvested layer in AI infrastructure.
